direct_push_containment_determination.classification_rationale: Ruleset 16177949 is active (main protected: true — live API); GH013 fires on direct push (live enforcement); bypass_actors list is empty (no actor has direct push capability); 100% of observable commits entered main via PR pathway
direct_push_containment_determination.classification_rule_applied: Ruleset active + No direct push actor → GOVERNED

If GitHub repo-owner native bypass exists outside the bypass_actors list, classification would be BREAK_GLASS_CONTAINED.

issue_1604_impact.rationale: This external verification resolves the PENDING_EXTERNAL_VERIFICATION closure state for direct_push_to_main and force_push_to_main — the two primary pathways flagged in the prior self-audit as requiring external GitHub configuration verification. Both pathways are now classifiable as GOVERNED based on live GitHub repository configuration evidence. The residual open items for issue #1604 are the status check wiring gaps (required checks not fully configured in the ruleset) and the proof_registry_bypass pending admin action — these are operational gaps, not admission pathway vulnerabilities.
remaining_open_gaps: required_status_checks not fully wired in ruleset 16177949 (admin config needed)
generate-merge-proof must be removed from required checks (deadlock risk)
proof_registry_bypass for github-actions[bot] not yet configured
